I'm a Senior Product Designer who turns complex, ambiguous product challenges into clear, elegant experiences. My process lives at the intersection of deep user understanding, business strategy, and technical feasibility.

With over 10 years of craft, I've navigated the complexities of SaaS ecosystems, enterprise workflows, and high-scale consumer products. My goal is simple: to make the most intricate technology feel inevitable and effortless for the end-user.

© 2026 Maayan Lazarovich
Maayan Lazarovich

Designing for
Impact & Scale.

Portrait placeholder
400 × 533 px

I'm a Senior Product Designer with over 10 years of experience turning complex, ambiguous challenges into clear, elegant digital products. Currently based in Tel Aviv, I help companies bridge the gap between user needs and business strategy.

Throughout my career, I've navigated the intricacies of B2B SaaS, enterprise ecosystems, and high-scale design systems. Whether it's a startup scaling its first MVP or an enterprise rethinking a legacy platform, my focus remains the same: functional minimalism.

I believe that the best design is often invisible—it removes friction so seamlessly that the user never notices the effort behind it. If I'm not pushing pixels, I'm likely questioning if a feature is truly necessary for the core experience.

Expert
Disciplines
Product Strategy & Systems Thinking
Designing cohesive systems and scaling complex product architectures to solve for user needs and business goals.
User-Centered Design & Research Integration
Translating diverse user insights, behavioral data, and research findings into actionable design decisions that drive adoption.
Team Leadership & Cross-Functional Collaboration
Championing a collaborative design culture and aligning multiple stakeholders, developers, and product managers toward a shared vision.

© 2026 Maayan Lazarovich
Financial Operations · 2025

Automating Financial
Operations

Streamlining complex B2B financial workflows through role-based automation and data clarity.

Lead Product Designer
Clarity
2025
Web (SaaS / FinTech)
A unified financial workspace designed for clarity, efficiency, and role-based automation.
On this page
  • Overview
  • The Challenge
  • Research
  • Design Process
  • The Solution
  • Outcome

Overview

Clarity is a data visualization platform built for teams who need to make sense of large, complex financial datasets without a data science background. Users were drowning in data but starving for insight.

“Users were drowning in data but starving for insight. We needed to flip the ratio.”

The Challenge

The existing product required users to build queries from scratch—technically correct but humanly exhausting. Onboarding drop-off was 68% within the first session. Even basic financial operations required navigating multiple nested menus before any action could be taken.

Analyzing task prioritization and identifying friction points. The complex dashboard data entry was a primary target for automation.

Research & Discovery

I ran 22 user interviews across analysts, managers, and executives. Each group needed a completely different entry point to the same data. Three distinct mental models emerged:

Analysts
Data First
Need raw access and filtering tools before any summary view.
Managers
Status First
Prioritize team performance, deadlines, and exception flags.
Executives
Insight First
Want high-level KPIs and trend signals, nothing else.

Design Process

I introduced a role-based onboarding system routing users to pre-configured dashboard templates based on job function. Rather than a blank canvas, each role arrived at a contextual starting point—reducing cognitive load from the first interaction.

Key design decisions included progressive disclosure of complex financial data, a task prioritization layer surfacing urgent items, and a bulk operations system that reduced repetitive manual entry.

Ensuring granular data precision. Every product attribute, from prices to SKUs, must be perfectly synced across the new interface.

The Solution: Bulk Automation

The design evolved through three major iteration cycles, each informed by usability testing with 8–12 participants. The final solution centered on a Bulk Update command center—allowing financial operators to execute complex data changes across hundreds of records simultaneously.

The finalized Bulk Update command center: Executing complex data changes across hundreds of records simultaneously, strictly Above the Fold.

Measurable Impact

The redesign delivered significant improvements across all key activation and retention metrics within the first 90 days of launch.

4.5min
Time-to-first-insight
(down from 45 min)
79%
Onboarding Completion
(up from 32%)
+34%
Enterprise Retention at 90 Days

AI Recruitment · 2023

Humanizing AI-Driven
Recruitment

Empowering teams with granular, intuitive control over AI-assisted hiring workflows.

Lead Product Designer
myInterview
2023
Web + Mobile
A high-level view of the new Users management dashboard — balancing dense data with clear visual hierarchy.
On this page
  • Overview
  • The Challenge
  • Features
  • Bulk Actions
  • Role Matrix
  • Outcome

Overview

myInterview is a video-based hiring platform for asynchronous candidate screening. I owned the end-to-end redesign of the team collaboration layer—building a Roles & Permissions system that scales from small startups to enterprise hiring teams without becoming overwhelming.

“AI tools in recruitment often feel like black boxes with rigid access levels. We needed to bring back human control.”

The Challenge

In fast-paced recruitment, AI tools often feel like ‘black boxes’ with rigid access levels. Teams needed a way to collaborate effectively while maintaining strict data privacy and role-specific workflows.

The goal: design a flexible Roles & Permissions system that scales from small startups to enterprise-level hiring teams without becoming overwhelming.

The Problem
Rigid, one-size-fits-all permission levels forced teams into workarounds. Admins couldn’t delegate without exposing sensitive candidate data.
The Goal
A flexible, transparent permission matrix that grows with the team—from 3 default roles to unlimited custom configurations.

Feature A — Intuitive Team Invitations

Streamlining the ‘Invite’ flow by allowing admins to assign roles and groups simultaneously, reducing friction in team onboarding. A recruiter can now be invited, permissioned, and placed in the right groups in a single action.

Invite flow combining role assignment and group allocation in a single step — reducing onboarding friction for enterprise teams.

Feature B — Visual Feedback & Quick Actions

A dynamic, fluid workspace—moving candidates and sharing jobs with one click. Real-time visual feedback ensures admins always know the system state without needing to refresh or navigate away.

The fluid collaborative workspace — moving candidates and sharing jobs with a single click, with real-time permission feedback.

Feature C — Bulk Actions & Permission Management

Implementing a clean Bulk Action Bar for managing multiple users or groups at once, ensuring high efficiency for power users. Selected rows reveal a context-sensitive action bar—floating above the table to stay within the viewport.

Bulk user selection with context-sensitive action bar — Set Permission, Assign to Groups, or Delete across multiple users simultaneously. Group management view with bulk selection — enabling admins to restructure team permissions at scale without repetitive manual edits.

Feature D — Granular Role Definition

A deep dive into the Permission Matrix view. I redesigned how permissions are toggled across Job, Invite, and Candidate management—providing total transparency on what each role can see and do. The column-based layout makes cross-role comparison effortless.

The permission matrix in edit mode — admins can precisely tune what each role can access across every product surface. Role context menu providing Rename, Duplicate, Restore Defaults, and Delete actions — enabling admins to manage custom roles without starting from scratch.

Measurable Impact

The Roles & Permissions redesign delivered measurable improvements in scalability, efficiency, and admin satisfaction across all enterprise customer segments.

01
Dynamic Engine
100% adaptability to any enterprise structure
40%
Faster Onboarding
via predefined role templates
95%
Admin Clarity
on granular permission matrix visibility

Contextual Design · 2022

Contextual Library
Experience

Senior Product Designer
Elementor
2022 – 2024
Web
On this page
  • Overview
  • The Challenge
  • Research
  • Design Process
  • Outcome

Scaling the Library Experience for 15M+ Websites.

Elementor powers over 15 million websites. I led the redesign of the editor's contextual library system—surfacing the right assets, blocks, and settings based on where the user is in the design process.

“15 million sites built on a panel system nobody fully understood. It was time to fix that.”

The Labyrinth of Settings

Over 6 years, the left panel grew into a labyrinth of nested settings. Panel-related support tickets accounted for 31% of all volume.

Data-Driven Insights

A 4-week discovery sprint: 1,200 session recordings, tree testing with 340 users, 14 in-depth interviews.

From Generic to Contextual

I designed a context-aware panel that surfaces the most relevant settings based on canvas selection. Average clicks: 6.2 → 2.1.

The ‘Customize Kit’ engine allowed for real-time brand try-ons before importing.

Measurable Impact

Redesigning the contextual library system delivered clear, measurable improvements.

44%
Drop in panel-related support tickets.
51%
New user activation rate.
(up from 29%)
3.2×
Increase in adoption of previously hidden settings.
